name: react-native
description: Command line tools that ship with react-native in form of the @react-native-community/cli package

options.name: --version
options.description: Print CLI version

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Increase logging verbosity

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

subcommands.name: doctor
subcommands.description: [EXPERIMENTAL] Diagnose and fix common Node.js, iOS, Android & React Native issues

options.name: --fix
options.description: Attempt to fix all diagnosed issues

options.name: --contributor
options.description: Add healthchecks required to installations required for contributing to React Native

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

subcommands.name: bundle
subcommands.description: Builds the javascript bundle for offline use

options.name: --entry-file
options.description: Path to the root JS file, either absolute or relative to JS root

options.name: --platform
options.description: Either "ios" or "android" (default: "ios")

options.name: --transformer
options.description: Specify a custom transformer to be used

options.name: --dev
options.description: If false, warnings are disabled and the bundle is minified (default: true)

options.name: --minify
options.description: Allows overriding whether bundle is minified. This defaults to false if dev is true, and true if dev is false. Disabling minification can be useful for speeding up production builds for testing purposes

options.name: --bundle-output
options.description: File name where to store the resulting bundle, ex. /tmp/groups.bundle

options.name: --bundle-encoding
options.description: Encoding the bundle should be written in (https://nodejs.org/api/buffer.html#buffer_buffer). (default: "utf8")

options.name: --max-workers
options.description: Specifies the maximum number of workers the worker-pool will spawn for transforming files. This defaults to the number of the cores available on your machine

options.name: --sourcemap-output
options.description: File name where to store the sourcemap file for resulting bundle, ex. /tmp/groups.map

options.name: --sourcemap-sources-root
options.description: Path to make sourcemap's sources entries relative to, ex. /root/dir

options.name: --sourcemap-use-absolute-path
options.description: Report SourceMapURL using its full path

options.name: --assets-dest
options.description: Directory name where to store assets referenced in the bundle

options.name: --unstable-transform-profile
options.description: Experimental, transform JS for a specific JS engine. Currently supported: hermes, hermes-canary, default

options.name: --reset-cache
options.description: Removes cached files

options.name: --read-global-cache
options.description: Try to fetch transformed JS code from the global cache, if configured

options.name: --config
options.description: Path to the CLI configuration file

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize a new React Native project named <projectName> in a directory of the same name

options.name: --version
options.description: Shortcut for `--template react-native@version`

options.name: --template
options.description: Uses a custom template. Valid arguments are the ones supported by `yarn add [package]` or `npm install [package]`, if you are using `--npm` option

options.name: --npm
options.description: Forces using npm for initialization

options.name: --directory
options.description: Uses a custom directory instead of `<projectName>`

options.name: --title
options.description: Uses a custom app title name for application

options.name: --skip-install
options.description: Skips dependencies installation step

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

options.name: --port
options.description: Port on which to listen to

options.name: --host
options.description: Change the default host

options.name: --projectRoot
options.description: Path to a custom project root

options.name: --watchFolders
options.description: Specify any additional folders to be added to the watch list

options.name: --assetPlugins
options.description: Specify any additional asset plugins to be used by the packager by full filepath

options.name: --sourceExts
options.description: Specify any additional source extensions to be used by the packager

options.name: --max-workers
options.description: Specifies the maximum number of workers the worker-pool will spawn for transforming files. This defaults to the number of the cores available on your machine

options.name: --transformer
options.description: Specify a custom transformer to be used

name: --reset-cache
--resetCache
options.description: Removes cached files

name: --custom-log-reporter-path
--customLogReporterPath
options.description: Path to a JavaScript file that exports a log reporter as a replacement for TerminalReporter

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enables logging

options.name: --https
options.description: Enables https connections to the server

options.name: --key
options.description: Path to custom SSL key

options.name: --cert
options.description: Path to custom SSL cert

options.name: --config
options.description: Path to the CLI configuration file

options.name: --no-interactive
options.description: Disables interactive mode

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

subcommands.name: ram-bundle
subcommands.description: Builds javascript as a "Random Access Module" bundle for offline use

options.name: --entry-file
options.description: Path to the root JS file, either absolute or relative to JS root

options.name: --platform
options.description: Either "ios" or "android" (default: "ios")

options.name: --transformer
options.description: Specify a custom transformer to be used

options.name: --dev
options.description: If false, warnings are disabled and the bundle is minified (default: true)

options.name: --minify
options.description: Allows overriding whether bundle is minified. This defaults to false if dev is true, and true if dev is false. Disabling minification can be useful for speeding up production builds for testing purposes

options.name: --bundle-output
options.description: File name where to store the resulting bundle, ex. /tmp/groups.bundle

options.name: --bundle-encoding
options.description: Encoding the bundle should be written in (https://nodejs.org/api/buffer.html#buffer_buffer). (default: "utf8")

options.name: --max-workers
options.description: Specifies the maximum number of workers the worker-pool will spawn for transforming files. This defaults to the number of the cores available on your machine

options.name: --sourcemap-output
options.description: File name where to store the sourcemap file for resulting bundle, ex. /tmp/groups.map

options.name: --sourcemap-sources-root
options.description: Path to make sourcemap's sources entries relative to, ex. /root/dir

options.name: --sourcemap-use-absolute-path
options.description: Report SourceMapURL using its full path

options.name: --assets-dest
options.description: Directory name where to store assets referenced in the bundle

options.name: --unstable-transform-profile
options.description: Experimental, transform JS for a specific JS engine. Currently supported: hermes, hermes-canary, default

options.name: --reset-cache
options.description: Removes cached files

options.name: --read-global-cache
options.description: Try to fetch transformed JS code from the global cache, if configured

options.name: --config
options.description: Path to the CLI configuration file

options.name: --indexed-ram-bundle
options.description: Force the "Indexed RAM" bundle file format, even when building for android

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

subcommands.name: link
subcommands.description: Links assets and optionally native modules

options.name: --platforms
options.description: Scope linking to specified platforms

options.name: --all
options.description: Link all native modules and assets

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrade your app's template files to the specified or latest npm version using `rn-diff-purge` project. Only valid semver versions are allowed

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Get relevant version info about OS, toolchain and libraries

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

options.name: --platforms
options.description: Scope unlinking to specified platforms

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

subcommands.name: profile-hermes
subcommands.description: Pull and convert a Hermes tracing profile to Chrome tracing profile, then store it in the directory <destinationDir> of the local machine

options.name: --filename
options.description: File name of the profile to be downloaded, eg. sampling-profiler-trace8593107139682635366.cpuprofile

options.name: --raw
options.description: Pulls the original Hermes tracing profile without any transformation

options.name: --sourcemap-path
options.description: The local path to your source map file, eg. /tmp/sourcemap.json

options.name: --generate-sourcemap
options.description: Generates the JS bundle and source map

options.name: --port
options.description: Default: "8081"

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

subcommands.name: run-android
subcommands.description: Builds your app and starts it on a connected Android emulator or device

options.name: --root
options.description: [DEPRECATED - root is discovered automatically] Override the root directory for the android build (which contains the android directory) (default: "")

options.name: --variant
options.description: Specify your app's build variant (default: "debug")

options.name: --appFolder
options.description: [DEPRECATED – use "project.android.appName" in react-native.config.js] Specify a different application folder name for the android source. If not, we assume is "app"

options.name: --appId
options.description: Specify an applicationId to launch after build. If not specified, `package` from AndroidManifest.xml will be used. (default: "")

options.name: --appIdSuffix
options.description: Specify an applicationIdSuffix to launch after build. (default: "")

options.name: --main-activity
options.description: Name of the activity to start (default: "MainActivity")

options.name: --deviceId
options.description: Builds your app and starts it on a specific device/simulator with the given device id (listed by running "adb devices" on the command line)

options.name: --no-packager
options.description: Do not launch packager while building

options.name: --port
options.description: Default: 8081

options.name: --terminal
options.description: Launches the Metro Bundler in a new window using the specified terminal path. (default: "Apple_Terminal")

options.name: --tasks
options.description: Run custom Gradle tasks. By default it's "installDebug"

options.name: --no-jetifier
options.description: Do not run "jetifier" – the AndroidX transition tool. By default it runs before Gradle to ease working with libraries that don't support AndroidX yet. See more at: https://www.npmjs.com/package/jetifier

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information

subcommands.name: run-ios
subcommands.description: Builds your app and starts it on iOS simulator

options.name: --simulator
options.description: Explicitly set simulator to use. Optionally include iOS version between parenthesis at the end to match an exact version: "iPhone 6 (10.0)" (default: "iPhone 12")

options.name: --configuration
options.description: Explicitly set the scheme configuration to use (default: "Debug")

options.name: --scheme
options.description: Explicitly set Xcode scheme to use

options.name: --project-path
options.description: Path relative to project root where the Xcode project (.xcodeproj) lives. (default: "ios")

options.name: --device
options.description: Explicitly set device to use by name

options.name: --udid
options.description: Explicitly set device to use by udid

options.name: --no-packager
options.description: Do not launch packager while building

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Do not use xcpretty even if installed

options.name: --port
options.description: Default: 8081

options.name: --terminal
options.description: Launches the Metro Bundler in a new window using the specified terminal path. (default: "Apple_Terminal")

name: -h
--help
options.description: Output usage information
